description:
You are analyzing a deep-field astronomical image to separate stars from galaxies based on morphological parameters. Using the image at `~/AstroImages/classification/deep_field.fits`, extract isolated sources and record their Area, Centroid, and Shape Descriptors (including Circularity), while excluding small noise artifacts. Save the resulting data to `~/AstroImages/classification/output/source_catalog.csv`. Based on the morphological data, define criteria to distinguish stars (compact and circular), galaxies (larger and less circular), and artifacts. Document your findings in a report at `~/AstroImages/classification/output/classification_report.txt` using the following format:

CRITERIA_STARS: [your reasoning here]
CRITERIA_GALAXIES: [your reasoning here]
CRITERIA_ARTIFACTS: [your reasoning here]
COUNT_STARS: [integer]
COUNT_GALAXIES: [integer]
COUNT_ARTIFACTS: [integer]
GALAXY_1_X: [X coordinate of a galaxy]
GALAXY_1_Y: [Y coordinate of a galaxy]
GALAXY_2_X: [X coordinate of a second galaxy]
GALAXY_2_Y: [Y coordinate of a second galaxy]
GALAXY_3_X: [X coordinate of a third galaxy]
GALAXY_3_Y: [Y coordinate of a third galaxy]

Ensure the report includes exact X and Y centroid coordinates for three distinct galaxies identified in your catalog.
